generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance InputCurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal sensor measuring the robot's input current"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InputCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance MotorAngularVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal sensor measuring the robot's motor angular velocity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorAngularVelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance FirstAxisPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal sensor measuring the robot's first‑axis position"
    DTDFVocab:producedData FirstAxisPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance FrictionMeasurementSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing measured joint friction values from the manipulator"
    DTDFVocab:producedData FrictionMeasurementDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance InputCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InputCurrentSensor
]

instance MotorAngularVelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorAngularVelocitySensor
]

instance FirstAxisPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FirstAxisPositionSensor
]

instance FrictionMeasurementDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom FrictionMeasurementSensor
]

// C10: Data (pre‑processed / generated)
instance PreprocessedCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered input current after preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance PreprocessedVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered motor angular velocity after preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorAngularVelocityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance PreprocessedPositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low‑pass filtered first‑axis position after preprocessing"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FirstAxisPositionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance RawFrictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw joint friction measurement obtained from the friction sensor"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionMeasurementDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance ProcessedFrictionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction value computed by white‑box and black‑box models for digital‑shadow storage"
    DTDFVocab:fromData FrictionMeasurementDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

// C10: Models
instance WhiteBoxFrictionPhysicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics‑based white‑box model representing internal and external joint friction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance BlackBoxFrictionNeuralNetworkModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data‑driven neural‑network model predicting friction from sensor history"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance RobotSysMLStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML block diagram describing the robot’s structural hierarchy"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance RobotSysMLBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML internal block diagram capturing the robot’s behavioral logic"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

instance SimulinkFrictionSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink simulation model used for offline friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AasExecutorEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance AasExecutorEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell (AAS) Executor subscribes to MQTT topics, interprets the AAS data model, builds the digital shadow and forwards data to ThingsBoard"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeFrictionMonitoringService,
                     FrictionCompensationService,
                     PredictiveMaintenanceService,
                     ProcessOptimizationService,
                     CO2ReductionService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeFrictionMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides continuous real‑time monitoring of joint friction values"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLevelInsight
]

instance FrictionCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes automatic friction compensation inside the robot control loop"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects early friction anomalies and triggers maintenance recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnomalyInsight,
                     MaintenanceSchedulingAction
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Runs simulation experiments on the twin to optimize machining parameters"
    DTDFVocab:provides MachiningParameterOptimizationInsight,
                     ParameterTuningAction
]

instance CO2ReductionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Supports CO₂‑reduction scenarios by tuning process parameters before production"
    DTDFVocab:provides CO2EmissionReductionInsight,
                     ProcessAdjustmentAction
]

// C17: Insights
instance FrictionLevelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current friction level insight derived from processed data"
]

instance FrictionAnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight indicating a potential abnormal increase in joint friction"
]

instance MachiningParameterOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight about optimal machining parameters obtained from twin simulations"
]

instance CO2EmissionReductionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight estimating CO₂ emissions reduction achievable by parameter tuning"
]

// C17: Actions
instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of control commands to compensate measured friction"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceSchedulingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Schedule maintenance activities based on predicted friction degradation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterTuningAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Apply optimized machining parameters to the robot controller"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ProcessAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjust process settings to meet CO₂ reduction targets"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The robot digital twin is required to operate in real‑time, synchronising the physical robot’s sensor stream with the friction simulation at the control‑loop rate. The system must keep the end‑to‑end latency within the dead‑time budget of the robot controller so that joint‑friction compensation can be applied during runtime. Data are streamed continuously via MQTT/ThingsBoard and processed by the simulation models, which are re‑executed each time a new data trace arrives."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The digital‑twin constellation follows a pipeline architecture: (1) physical robot publishes raw sensor data over MQTT to a ThingsBoard IoT platform; (2) a processing component ingests the data, feeds it into executable simulation models (white‑box physics or black‑box neural nets) implemented in Simulink and exposed via FMI; (3) results are stored in the robot’s Asset Administration Shell; (4) a visualization dashboard reads the AAS/ThingsBoard data. The orchestration is model‑driven, using reference‑model concepts (digital shadow reference model) and component‑connector ADL ideas to ensure traceability of each data trace to its generating processing component and models."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin lifecycle described in the documents follows the MBSE abstraction hierarchy and the Digital‑Shadow reference model. It starts with a purpose‑driven conceptual stage where the need for a digital shadow/twin is defined (purpose‑driven design). Next, a modeling stage creates abstract models using SysML, UML, or ADLs (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc) to capture requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture and product/technical layer. A concretization stage then maps the reference model concepts to concrete classes and associations, producing a conforming class diagram. Afterwards a processing stage introduces Processing components that consume input data traces, apply simulation or data‑calculation models, and generate output traces, preserving traceability. Finally, the operational stage runs the instantiated twin, continuously ingesting one‑way data from the physical asset and optionally feeding back decisions. Transition between stages is managed through the “originates‑from” association that maintains provenance of data traces, and governance is implied by the conformance relation that defines which concrete models are admissible."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured by traceability and verification of all transformations from high‑level SysML specifications to simulation or data‑driven models; correctness of these transformations is verified according to methods proposed for model‑based development, and uncertainty is addressed by linking each data trace to its generating processing component and input traces, allowing error detection and correction. The reliability of the digital shadow depends on the correctness of the underlying white‑box (Simulink) and black‑box (machine‑learning) models, with verification steps that confirm that the models accurately represent the physical phenomena before they are integrated into the Twin."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connectivity relies on an MQTT broker that mediates communication between the robot’s preprocessing component and the digital‑shadow services. MQTT provides a lightweight, message‑based, publish‑subscribe architecture using hierarchical topics; messages are untyped JSON payloads. The AAS Executor and the ThingsBoard visualization platform both act as MQTT clients subscribing to the relevant topics. While the documents do not specify bandwidth, latency, or QoS levels, the architecture follows a star‑topology centered on the broker, with support for read/write permission assignments on topics to ensure controlled access."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The digital‑shadow pipeline is deployed on an on‑premise infrastructure that includes a configured relational database for the DS data model, an MQTT broker, an AAS Executor component, and a ThingsBoard instance for visualization. The Asset Administration Shell serves as the single source of truth, encapsulating the data model, sensor mappings, and links to simulation FMU files. No explicit cloud or edge platforms are mentioned; the system is hosted locally, with scalability implied through the use of standard components (e.g., MQTT and ThingsBoard) that can be scaled horizontally as needed."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is achieved through MQTT as the publish‑subscribe protocol for sensor data, ThingsBoard for IoT data storage and visualization, and the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) as the Industry 4.0‑standard wrapper that aggregates the SysML structural model and FMI‑wrapped simulation models. The twin therefore interoperates with enterprise IT layers (e.g., dashboards, optimisation services) and can be extended with further digital‑shadow instances via the same AAS‑based APIs."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The documents reference several industry modeling and communication standards that are adopted for the Digital Twin. Structural models use UML class diagrams, SysML block definition & internal block diagrams, and architecture description languages such as ACME, AADL, and MontiArc, which are standardized modelling notations. Data exchange is based on the MQTT protocol (Mosquitto implementation) and the ThingsBoard IoT platform, both conforming to OASIS MQTT v5 specifications. The Digital Shadow reference model itself is expressed as a UML class diagram, providing a standardised metamodel for digital shadow concepts. Conformance relations defined in reference modeling (per [41]) serve as a formal compliance framework ensuring that concrete twins adhere to the specified standards."
]
